pageTitle.value: Jiří Kylián and Jason Akira Somma - Video

pageCaption.value: Jason Akira Somma describes himself as “a dance visual artist”, transcending artistic boundaries. In Jiří Kylián he finds not only a mentor, but also a generous “life coach” happy to share a lifetime’s experience. The mentorship, says Somma, “has absolutely changed my life”.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Dance, 2008 - 2009

title.value: Jiří Kylián and Jason Akira Somma

text.value: Jason Akira Somma describes himself as “a dance visual artist”, transcending artistic boundaries. In Jiří Kylián he finds not only a mentor, but also a generous “life coach” happy to share a lifetime’s experience. The mentorship, says Somma, “has absolutely changed my life”.

pageCaption.value: Russian-born choreographer Alexei Ratmansky and his protégé Myles Thatcher share a deep respect for the “language” that is classical dance. Both are intensely involved in reinvigorating their art form for the 21st century. A perfect partnership.

metadescription.value: Russian-born choreographer Alexei Ratmansky and his protégé Myles Thatcher share a deep respect for the “language” that is classical dance. Both are intensely involved in reinvigorating their art form for the 21st century. A perfect partnership.

pageTitle.value: Lin Hwai-min and Eduardo Fukushima - Video

pageCaption.value: Young Brazilian dancer Eduardo Fukushima moves from São Paulo to Taiwan, where his mentor, Asia’s premier choreographer Lin Hwai-min, elaborates his unique philosophy of movement and being. A year with this “very generous person” has “transformed my life”, Fukushima declares.

WaPageTitle.value: Lin Hwai-min and Eduardo Fukushima - Video

pageTitle.value: William Forsythe and Sang Jijia - Video

pageCaption.value: William Forsythe, one of the greatest innovators of modern dance, believes that ballet “shouldn’t be left in the 19th century”. His radical approach finds the perfect protégé in Sang Jijia, a gifted Chinese dancer of Tibetan origin. “Sang-ba is like unpolluted water,” says Forsythe. “He runs clear.”

WaPageTitle.value: William Forsythe and Sang Jijia - Video

pageTitle.value: Martin Scorsese and Celina Murga - Video

pageCaption.value: For American master of cinema Martin Scorsese, to be a good film director, you need to become part of the world you are filming. His protégée, Argentinean director Celina Murga, does so with passion, he declares, describing her as a natural film-maker. The admiration is mutual. “Marty’s the perfect mentor,” says Murga.

WaPageTitle.value: Martin Scorsese and Celina Murga - Video

pageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageCaption.value: Declaring their mentorship to be “a process of development”, Wole Soyinka, first African Laureate of the Nobel Prize for Literature, encourages his protégée, Tara June Winch, as she agonizes over her writing. When anyone writes, he says, himself included, “it’s all over the place.” It’s the rewriting that follows that marks out a true writer.

WaPageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageTitle.value: Youssou N’Dour and Aurelio Martínez - Video

pageCaption.value: A year’s mentorship with Youssou N’Dour gives Aurelio Martínez, from Honduras, not only an opportunity to boost his musical skills and self-confidence in the company of “the icon of African music”, but also to learn more about the historic and musical background of his African ancestors as he visits N’Dour’s continent for the first time.
